comments.text: Amazing professor!!

Dr. Ouda is good for CS 3330 and 4050, but I recommend taking 4320 with JimR (fall semesters). His teaching style is pretty good and the assignments are reasonable in theory, but the TAs are incredibly nit-picky on the grading of assignments. The exams test memorization of the slides, so literally memorize everything he says is important.

Ouda is one of the most overhated profs in the CS department. He is very well organized, with lots of detailed slides that lay out the content very well. He gets excited any time someone has a question in class. He's a tough grader for attention to detail on homework. Tests follow examples from the slides and require that you write code by hand.

Good Luck. You can't understand what he is saying half the time, he covers hundreds of slides and then doesn't post the examples that are test relevant. Does not test over understanding of a topic, but your ability to copy and paste code from his slides. Then gets mad at the class for performing poorly instead of taking any of the fault.

Dr. Ouda is a pretty good teacher for OOP. This is the first CS class where I've been able to understand all the content really easily and score high scores on all the quizzes and exams. My only complaint is his TAs grade pretty harshly for things that are not significant such as messing up a print statement. Other than that this was a good class.

The group assignments were graded harshly, made worse by the fact that no one ever explained how we were supposed to complete them. He insists the class “isn't based on memorization,” yet every exam demands exactly that. Honestly, there are far too many issues to list here. I do NOT have a grasp on software development practices after attending.
Dd not grade based on any rubric for a single assignment, and scoring was never transparent. In fact, ta made several mistakes on different assignments and exams/quizzes.
The class, being a 3-credit course, should be a crime itself with the amount of workload in his SWE class.

J2100 is a lot of work, and Prof. Romero is a very strict grader. AP Style understanding is a must PRIOR to taking the course - they don't teach it before you are graded on it. Instructions were clear, but grading criteria is unnecessarily harsh.

I loved Romero. Yes, her grading was hard but she mirrors the real journalism world. I got 100% on 2 major projects. You just have to work your @$$ off and you'll be fine. She taught me so much and her grading helped me be way better prepared for other classes.

Romero was okay. She had fair grading, but it was pretty harsh. You can easily lose up to half your points off an assignment due to AP style errors or deadlines. Even though AP style errors are not tolerated in the journalism field, I did think it the grading criteria was a bit exaggerated in the beginning of the course.

She definitely is a tough grader in the beginning of the semester. But once you get to know her she gives good feedback and wants you to better your writing style. Cannot miss lab at all. Most writing is done outside of class. She is a big fan of peer editing papers and will sometimes give out 2 bonus points for doing so.

She is a very tough grader, be prepared to think you are not a good writer. She will tell you how she feels, too. DO NOT take her unless you are thick-skinned. Be prepared to read all of the materials. She has a bad side, don't get on it because you will not pass.  Make sure you know AP style because she makes it to where it is make or break you.

She is one of the toughest graders I have ever had. She is nit picky about every little detail. She is available for her office hours, but if you can't make them, don't expect to get much help outside of class. If you have never taken a real journalism class like me, I would try to avoid her.

If you take J2100 Lab with Anna, you need to realize from the very beginning that the class won't be about whether or not you're a good writer, all she cares about is AP Style errors.  You're better off writing moderately well with basic facts and minor details but perfect styling than writing a nobel prize winning story with a few AP errors. Ehhhh
Talented woman, but condescending and not that helpful. If you do well on AP style, odds are you'll get a good grade.

This class is tough. The homework takes forever and the exams are difficult, but the prof gives us lots of tools to prepare us. He asks for feedback all the time and obviously wants us to do well. He's a good guy

Balal is an awesome professor and so clearly cares about his students! He's very understanding and willing to meet students where they're at. As a non-major, accounting is not my thing, but I would highly recommend taking his class. He's happy to set up one on ones to help you understand better and gives opportunities for extra credit too!

Very personable and approachable. Mandatory class, consists of slides and a group activity. Tedious/annoying to do activities when we go over everything together at the end. Comp check every two weeks which is the most annoying part of this course. “Open note” but uses lockdown browser and answers aren't in the textbook or hardly ever the notes.

One of the best professors I have ever had.  Lectures are very thorough and highly interactive. Expectations are clear, and will never throw a curve ball. As long as you attend lecture and lab, you will do just fine.

She is so nice, she makes lecture super interactive which is great, prepare to show up because there are opportunities for points randomly throughout lecture. Such a sweet woman, wish I had her next semester.
Going to labs and lectures is the only way you will pass!

You really don't learn much from the lectures so once you get your clicker points you don't have to go. Just make sure you know how to do the homework and look at the sample exams and you'll be fine for the exams.

Lectures are awful but lab is actually useful. I learned everything in lab and basically taught myself the rest. Easy to learn though.  She tries to teach during lecture but I did not learn a single thing in it.

Billie's class was great. Her exams are almost directly from the material learned in class. Go to lecture, go to lab and you'll be fine. Mostly just basic math. She does take attendance so go to lecture to get easy points.
Pretty easy just go to lab and lecture and you will do fine

Billie loves her class and her topic, but the class itself sucks.  The TA's save your grade during lab as that's where I learned most of the content.  Tests are 90% of your grade and there are only three so they are not exceptionally hard, but screw up once and there goes your A.

I have never disliked a teacher more than this man in my life. You will spend every week doing the longest most meaningless assignments and painfully sitting through mindless lectures. He runs a backwards classroom & never says that, so be prepared to teach yourself all of the material. Do yourself a favor and NEVER take a class with Bruce Runyan.

Genuinely one of the worst professors I've had at Mizzou. iClicker is worth a huge chunk of your final grade but half the class gets every question wrong. Lectures are all over the place and he never teaches material, just does problems in excel with little no explanation. Lot's of homework and McGraw Hill questions that are never on the exams.

Great guy, tries to make the lecture enjoyable. However, he is all over the place. Excel, note-taking, Icilcker (that he gives you 15 seconds to answer), and just him talking about 8 different things, the lecture isn't beneficial. I had to teach myself everything using the practice exam. Study halls with the TA are more beneficial than he is

Entire class is McGraw Hill, and he doesn't actually teach anything, just does excel problems on the board. Expects you to learn everything from the ebook. Homework is ridiculously long and tedious, hours a week. Tracks iClicker in class, grades iClicker on correctness, and routinely at least half the class gets it wrong.

Nice guy but bad class structure. 3 exams in this class worth most of your grade. He doesn't teach any content, just uses excel to go through problems which does not teach you anything. Homework can be long and tedious. Need a restructure to this class ASAP

You will end up teaching yourself all the material because he doesn't lecture on the material, just does examples on excel and jokes around. We learn all problems on excel and then cant use it to take any exams. You have chat the homework or it takes 5 hours. 

He's a great guy who cares about his students future, and he's actually pretty funny

He is a nice and enthusiastic dude, but his teaching style can be very unorganized. Do all of the homework and get an 100% on them (it is impossible not to unless you don't finish), go to class, and average a 57% on the exams, and you will get a C-. If you just want to pass classes and don't particularly care about your GPA, you can pass easily.

Hear me out, he is a very interesting dude but he actually truly cares about every student. He is such a good dude and he wants the best for every student. He is always offering help for students. While his class is difficult, he really wants the best for everyone. Just show up to class and you'll be chillin!

Quizzes matter so much!! Please lock in on those early because I didn't. Focus on quizzes and short answer possibilities on the tests and you'll do pretty good on finals! Just TAKE NOTES every week for sure.

He's pretty good -- just make sure to go to lecture or at least go over the slides that are in lecture, because those are important for exams. If you fumble an exam it's fairly difficult to get your grade back up due to the class having no weights based on assignment type. Not a very hard class tho & you get a double sided cht-sheet for each exam.

I loved this course. Dr. P was very passionate about what he was teaching and the lecture notes were easy to follow. You will have around 6 hours of coursework to do outside of class (homework, concepts for the chapter, etc). Make sure to keep up with it and go to lecture (though its not mandatory) as stuff said in lecture may show up on the test!

Professor P is by far my favorite professor I have ever had. He makes his lectures engaging despite the dry subject material. If you are paying attention in lectures and labs there is no reason you should not get an A. That being said, although attendance is not required for lecture, DO NOT SKIP as you will be far better off in this class.

Don't get me wrong, he's a great genuine professor who actually does a good job helping us learn accounting. My problem is his 200 point exams are super hard with open ended problems being completely different from what he mentions in class. Also quizzes are more trivial based than what is actually covered on the test. Journal entries are so rough.

Dr. P was such a good professor. You actually learn and understand the content during lecture. Lecture attendance isn't required, but he gives really good examples of exam problems you could possibly see, so I definitely recommend going. Utilize cheat sheets and lab time!

Missed exam due to illness, awaiting response from professor as to whether or not I may retake the exam. If able to retake the exam I will end the course with a B+\A-, is the professor does not allow me to take the exam I will end with a D. This should not be legal.

Most important thing is to know how to do the lab problems, as they are most of the exams. The multiple choice questions are from the textbook so you actually have to read the book. Quizzes in lab also come from the textbook and are multiple choice.

Know how to do the lab problems and you will be fine.
Dr. Klein is not a fan of teaching this course so he is kind of nonchalant about it. The content was very easy to follow and the exams were not bad at all. Pretty easy class.

Linear Op is rough in the beginning but gets better. Klein made the class interesting and fun. Homeworks were never returned so not sure if they were graded or not. Klein is a lot of fun and really understands the content. Good class.

Klein is the best. He brings treats every Wednesday which rocks. The course content was interesting if you like algebra. The first exam was extremely hard, but they get better if you study hard. No notes posted online, go to every class! Klein grades on your average performance and attendance so show up! Awesome and very smart guy. Do the homework!
No Comments
Great professor for International Accounting. The course was pretty easy and informative. I would recommend.
It's been one of my favorite online classes. He's pretty cute too.

Dr. Lee is a good professor that makes you work for your grade, this class isn't an easy A. I asked a lot of stupid questions but he was very helpful. There is a lot of writing in this class don't' wait to the last minute. The midterm in hard but the final is way easier. I failed midterm but got a 89 on midterm.

Dr. Lee's Biochem Class is tough and requires a ton of work. That being said, if you enjoy science it is a outstanding class to take and you will improve your knowledge a ton. He ended up being one of my favorite professors. Quizzes every week over the past lecture. Exams are easy. Lab reports are long. He offers an abundant amount of extra credit.

Dr. Lee is an intelligent guy, this class and lab was the hardest one I've ever taken.  He makes you work for the grade and doesn't show you any mercy.  If you don't pass, it's not his fault.  Make sure you study hard and go to him for help if needed.  The lab can be fun but the reports are so involved and tedious.

Dr. Lee is one of my favorite professors on campus.  He is awesome, and shares awesome stories & is crystal clear with explanations.    That being said, anyone taking Biochem_2480 should know that the 2 credits require about 12 hours of work outside of the lab.    He gave free points to honors students, which was unfair but manageable.
Dr. Lee is a great instructor. Very helpful when I asked a lot of dumb questions. He really pushes you to understand experiments and to be a better writer. Overall a cool dude.

Dale is honestly a great professor, the final was due for this class early in the morning and I missed it and he allowed me to retake it later on in the evening get full credit. Honestly, one of the best professors ever.

Took this class online and overall a pretty solid class! I like an online class that has clear structure and this class is that! Notes and Quiz boom you're done for the week. There is a midterm and final test AND project but it was nothing crazy at all. A super solid online course if you're someone like me who prefers online!

Professor does not respond to students, however I hear that he is responsive on LinkedIn so there's that. Easy class overall though just don't expect to get any help from the professor, only the PLA's.

The entire course is online with modules coming every week. The grade for coding assignments is for participation and not if you did it correctly. The Quizes and Exams are extremely easy. If you have half a brain and can use chatgpt you will pass this class with an easy A.

Really well-formatted courses. He provides a lot of documentation for every module and grades weekly assignments based on effort. Reading the documentation takes a while but makes the quizzes a lot easier. Quizzes and tests are open note, with no time limit. Stay on track with the work and the course will be easy.

Extremely easy course if you already know Python - otherwise, you might have to do a lot of self teaching. Weekly workload was only about 30mins - 1hr for the first half, and then picked up to ~2-6hrs/week for the second half. Only complaint is the lack of consistent communication from Dale himself.

Dale is the best professor I have ever had hands down. If you have the chance to take a class with him definitely do. He cares about the students and actually wants us to learn. This class was completely self paced, as long as everything is completed by the end of the semester. He grades quickly and fairly and gives great feedback.

If you have the opportunity to learn Swift with Dale "The Alpha Male" Musser, you've gotta do it. This course, albeit online and asynchronous, is a step up from other CS and IT courses offered at Mizzou. Dale makes it a point to make sure you learn to program well and have fun while doing so, which is something that few professors manage to do.
I learned a lot from this course.
The professor is very knowledgeable.

Unapproachable, basically refused to help me when I tried to get help. Lectures were all the exact same and none of it was actually relevant to homework, projects, or the programming itself. Dan was extremely difficult to understand. All homework is group work, and she refused to acknowledge or help when I told her that I was the only active member
Great professor. lectures can be pretty boring, but she gives great resources and helps understand very well.

Lectures were definitely dry, but the syllabus was more to blame than her teaching. She took feedback and did her best to make sure we were happy with how she ran the course, which is all you can really ask for. Exams were never a surprise or a curveball, last two labs are super time consuming but lab is ran by TAs, not the prof.

Deniz is a very kind professor and cares about the success of her students! Make sure to show up for the biweekly quizzes, she'll cover the material on the quiz right before it. The labs are a good portion of your grade, but it's easy to get 100s on if you put in the work. The exams are all multiple choice with 10 built in bonus points.

Fair and kind professor. She genuinely wants you to succeed and is happy to meet with you to help. Lectures are not the best, but you can understand enough to easily do well on exams. She offered extra credit too. The labs are a different story as the manuals were not clear about what needed to be included, and the TAs are on a power trip.

Good professor, expectations were clear. Grading system is great. She's not great at lecturing and the lectures are pretty dry, but the class was overall somewhat easy if you have any prior knowledge of programming in any language. Exams have some trick questions, similar to the other professors of this course. But otherwise an overall good prof.

Not bad, I knew exactly what was expected of me. Very poor at lecturing incredibly dry but attendance is not mandatory and the class pace is so brutally slow you can learn it all in a few hours on your own before the exams and do well. Gani, one of the TAs, is on.a SERIOUS power trip, don't let him touch anything you need graded.

This class relies on C, which is not beginner friendly despite this being a 101 class. She writes quickly, which is a double-edged sword. I really like that she drops the lowest 2 labs, and the adjustable grading system. If you are not a CS major, take CS-1700, its the same concepts in a friendly-ier language. I wished the lectures weren't as dry.
The GOAT !!!!!!! Very nice guy and very accomdating
Outdated teaching style. Lectures were also very difficult to understand and in order to understand I would have to look up a youtube video/websites to understand the topics.

Lectures were amazing, but not necessary to attend. The textbook also does a great job of explaining the algorithms and data structures in this course. Tests aren't overwhelmingly difficult, but the time limits can be a crunch. Not a lot of homework. Lots of extra credit are provided at "Mock Interviews," which are just a glorified trivia bowl.

This class is breezy compared to 1050 and 2050, because it's barely even about coding, just algorithms. The professor does a great job stepping through every algorithm shown to make sure everyone understands.

From what I've heard definitely the better professor to take for this class (3050) but still may be a tough course for some people. Goes more in depth into theoretical concepts than you'll have had in previous classes, but that's part of what CS is. Homework isnt bad if you pay attention, definitely helps to read the textbook in addition to lecture
Does not know what hes talking about

Doug isn't the best lecturer.  The exams are really difficult but he does give more than generous curves.  The semester long project is confusing at times but he's more than willing to help along the way.

one of the worst profs ive ever had, gives wrong advice for the simulation, talks super slow and for entirety of class, confusing/bad explanations, his grading system is slow and confusing, p chill final presentation grade, but FAT final- he recommends you take at least 3 hours (its another simulation). bro also has assigned seats

It's insane how bad his advice is for the simulation. I was on our finance and listened to him; we underwent an impossible to recover from catastrophe and received a grade so low it hurt my insides. This was all his fault and I felt completely misguided and alone. Thank you professor.

Whole class is based on how well you do the simulation, but his guidance is faulty and you're pretty much thrown into the fire. Feel free to google as much as possible. Lectures are long and he grades stuff as late as possible because he doesn't have a TA. Not his fault + hes nice but this isn't what I thought capstone would be honestly

Doug is just not a good professor. He talks for the entire hour 15 and doesn't stop. He will give advice on the capstone simulation and if it doesn't help, he just says whoops. Also the final takes 3-6 hours usually. Nice guy, just really bad class. Take the other professor.
Doesnt communicate well with students. Doesnt understand how to use canvas DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR

Prof. Moesel is very thorough in how he grades assignments. It would be a challenging task given that he grades something like 70 students' assignment s himself without a TA. The class is so so and most students would go through the motion anyway but he showed good work ethics.

As a senior undergrad, Dr. Ekin has easily been my favorite professor. He is very knowledgeable, and does an excellent job at sharing said knowledge with his students. The homework assignments can be a bit tough, but Dr. Ekin is always readily available to help you out. He is also a nice and funny guy, which makes lectures enjoyable to attend!

Positives:
Labs are very well set up and explained. I was never confused.
Engages with students and is good at taking questions.
Tests are intuitive and don't seem unfair.
Negatives:
Just reads off slides a bit too much. Need more examples
We never get our tests back. Just goes over the whole test.

Ekin seems like a great guy who is very knowledgeable about the field. If you have any sort of coding experience in any language the class will be cake for you. Attendance isn't "mandatory" but are often pop quizzes that are only accessible in the classroom. Grades are largely made up of just a few exams that are all 40 question multiple choice.

I wish there was more material put out for exam specific review rather than just the lecture slides, I understand that is our job to create guides and such but the slides themselves seemed so hard to pull information off of without the context of the lectures. Great guy and very understanding of uncontrollable circumstances.

Ekin knows what he's doing, but it's hard to take notes during lectures because he goes so fast and mainly focuses on coding examples.  If you make an effort to know what you're doing, then you'll be fine.    Attendance is important, so be ready for iClicker quizzes.

Labs and tests can be hard sometimes but honestly I think the first exam and some of the earlier labs purely exist to weed people out. Ekin uses JimR's exams so be prepared for some annoying questions. The final was quite a bit easier compared to all the exams so just stick with the class and go to office hours if needed and you'll pass.
not a hard class but not an easy class either

Overall, I think Dr. Ekin is a good professor. He is very passionate about computer science and you can tell he loves languages like C and C++ and is always willing to answer questions. The labs are pretty fun and engaging. The tests are pretty difficult but not impossible. I prepared for the tests by testing random questions I had about C.

Dr. Mauldin's course is challenging but she is very clear about expectations and a fair grader. Attendance is not technically mandatory but if you are on the edge of a grade and you came to class all semester she will boost your grade.

She is clearly knowledgeable with what she talks about, however it's also clear she worked in the field first as she is not a very good teacher. Most of her class was throwing you head first into things with very little information or tutorial and expecting a high level of competency. Lectures to fast with very little notes you can take.

She is a good professor and knows what she is talking about, but you will like her or not based on whether you find the course material interesting, which many do not. She works through things really quick so do not fall behind, catching up is not a simple task.

Elaine seems to be the premier AIS professor at mizzou.  If you want to earn an IS certificate you will undoubtedly find yourself in a few of her courses. Although some of the concepts these courses cover can be a bit difficult, she sets clear, reasonable expectations. And believe it or not, you will use this stuff in the field.

Dr. Wang seems like a nice person, but I find her explanations difficult to understand. I struggle to grasp about 80% of what she's saying in class. As a result, I've had to rely heavily on independent research and online tutorial videos to keep up. It's frustrating because I genuinely want to learn the material, not just pass the class.

Dr. Wang  is a bad lecturer in that she is monotone, very easy to tune out, and not terribly good at explaining what things are or why.  All the assignments: copy from book, then add book's suggested next steps. Midterm and final: copy a weird game she found online. No creativity involved. If you know Unity skip. If you don't, learn online and skip

By far the worst professor I have ever had. The class is online, and there is a heavy language barrier, I cannot understand 70% what she is saying through the webcam mic. The material is outdated, and if you have bad PLAs, good luck because you are gonna have to teach yourself everything. If this class wasn't required I would have dropped it.

These ratings seem a bit harsh. I've had Professor Wang for 2810 and 3630, and they both went well. If you're having trouble with anything, she's usually able to accommodate. 3630 projects can get a bit tough, but you'll be fine as long as you put in the work.

VR Development was honestly one of the better classes this department offers, but it is challenging and it is a lot of work for 3 credit hours. There is also a decent language barrier so lectures were hard to follow at times. You do learn a lot about Unity and VR which is cool. In person demos for every assignment were annoying and rarely flexible.

This class had a lot of lectures and documentation, and was sometimes hard to know what was worth watching or reading and what wasn't. Some assignments are useful, but some aren't. Overall, it's a decent class and she is a good person.

I don't understand how Professor Wang has such a low rating. She is a good professor who is very passionate about the subject and gives a plethora of extra credit opportunities. Sure her exams are tough, but they are not heavily weighted and if you show up to class and pay attention you should do fine throughout. Her labs even have video guides.
good and has TA's as well a uses discord for questions

What he asks for and what he wants aren't always the same. When asked a question, he rarely answers what was asked. His talks are ridiculous: rambles on about semi-related things, is unclear about important concepts, etc.

I did not like his teaching style. He jumps around way too much and is very confusing. He repeats himself a lot saying how he smart and humble he is. These are the only two lines I remember from his class. Did not learn much at all.

he's very nice, smart and humbled. really liked the capstone course. besides, i was considering to apply for mba after a couple of years. i requested a meeting with Dr S and he spent 2 hours helping me with gmat questions. idk any prof other than Dr S who would do that for a student. in short, amazing guy and teacher.

I really loved his course and teaching. I think the person (very clear is the same one from the writing) is talking about someone else. He never talked about him, treats students fairly, cares about students. Exam was hard but his curve was super generous. Nobody I know got below B.
Do not take this professor. He is so unorganized and lost and doesn't tell you what he expects from you. He hasn't taught me much other than about himself. I was so willing to learn.

Too arrogant and isn't very clear or helpful. From his boring lectures, he already expects you to understand all the concepts. Rather than helping, he'll ridicule and chastise you for not knowing. It's hard to tell whether he's using constructive criticism, or just too mean and arrogant.

Professor Felipe is a great teacher who really cares about his students and always finds time to talk when requested. Not a fan of the capstone course because it is more like a finance course? but the prof's attitude and passion to teach is remarkable. Plus, he helped me with case questions for my consulting interview.
Best professor I had at Mizzou. The complaints below are false. How can a prof treat students badly if the classes were pre-recorded?
One of the few professors that cares about student success

Prof. Gary is pretty nice and flexible with students. I really appreciate how he ran both Capstone classes in that he gave us class time back to us to work on our projects. He was available outside of class time to help my group test our project and was able to answer all of our questions. Couldn't have asked for a better professor for this class.

Professor McKenzie is funny and really cares about students. I feel like his feedback and grading is very thorough and always well-thought-out. He is very accessible outside of class and I liked how he adjusted due dates and is in general a flexible person. I always liked how he ended class early and didn't have class when we had group meetings.

The most laid back and real prof in the CS department. Gary's really transparent about what's expected and sets clear deadlines for everything. 10/10 would get a beer with this absolutely beautiful man
Great teacher, gets concepts across well. Very available outside of class.

Gary is an absolute gem of a professor. This man very clearly cares about his students and constantly pushes them towards success. He's very understanding if/when failure rears its ugly head. He is very charismatic and an overall joy to work with for Capstone. I would give him a 6/5 if I could.

Capstone 2 is more of the same. Easygoing professor, difficult project work outside of class. He really cares about sending off good developers into the world. Don't stress the presentation at the end, just make sure you have the bases covered. If there's a glaring fault, make sure you're ready to handle questions about it. Work hard, this is it!

Garys Capstone class feels like a stereotypical college class Easygoing casual in-class conversations sitting on the side of a desk,with large timeconsuming papers and documentation outside of class. Find a good group to tell you when attendance is happening,and this class will reward you if your project is decent. Just say no to Machine Learning!

As it is blatantly obvious by everyone else, this professor is the worst I've had at Mizzou.  I'm sure the topics would actually be interesting and a lot more engaging (which is why I originally was drawn to this course) had it not been for his confusing lectures and terrible grading.  Really wish I would've come here first before making up my mind

I have so many thoughts about DeSouza and this course (all of them negative). In fact, my last review I left for him got deleted because I broke the guidelines. Long story short, don't take this class or any taught by him. He is a horrible professor, and this class really should be 6-8 credit hours. It's just not worth your mental health...

When there was an issue regarding low grades in the class, he once said "its rather because of the students, the class (material), or the degree program". Little does he seem to realize, it was because of him.

Horrible, and every other synonym you could think of. Gave a final project to potentially replace an exam score, but no grade was ever put in (this is also true for the final exam). Any friendly demeanor you get on first encounter is just a facade. Don't ever take if you can, don't be like most of us early on thinking naively "its not so bad".

If you are not perfect (which would include reading his mind) then he will treat you like a child. Beware he blames students for his mistakes and errors, even when there have been repeated instances back to back. He single-handedly caused me to go back into therapy within two assignments, so have extra cash on hand for that

I don't often write rate my professor scores, let alone negative ones. I do not say it lightly, this is the single worst class and professor ive taken at Mizzou. There are no redeeming qualities about this class. He expects work that would be acceptable for a 6-8 credit hour class. Lectures were chicken scratch and incomprehensible. Avoid all costs
Lectures are somewhat disorganized. Homework is somewhat disorganized. The class is somewhat disorganized. And labs are quite time consuming.
Do not take his class, he make it very hard.

3 Exams and 9 ch quizzes. He is clear on what will be on the exam and provides a sample quiz that's similar to actual quiz. He only pulls in-class examples on the exam, nothing we haven't seen before. He makes himself available so use it! Lectures are bland,I take notes on the lecture slides he gives, will help when you review (so go to class).

Kyung's lectures were difficult for me to follow because many slides were read directly without much additional explanation. In class, examples were often solved quickly without walking through the reasoning, so I felt I had to teach myself much of the material outside class.

Amazing professor for Operating Systems. Very engaging lectures and connects class topics to stories from his experience. Assignments can be tough though, but he provides tests that show exactly what your grade would be, so as long as you start early its hard to not get an A.

Awesome as a person but bad professor. Sometimes would not post the prelabs until the day before the lab. When asked about practices exam he would say that he doesn't have any since he hasn't been teaching the class for too long, but he wrote all the exams from scratch. This is not a proper excuse.
He's the reason why I'm changing majors.
Amazing professor

He's one of the funniest and most engaging teachers I've ever had. He knows how to get students engaged and gives a lot of examples to really get you to understand. The labs start getting a bit hard but not really as long as you do pre labs

This was my second go around at 1050 and to say the course was WORSE my second time around is an understatement. Ries is an ok professor and the lectures are helpful but the class is very test-heavy. tests were extremely difficult and was truthfully a luck based game. There was never a clear cut answer and you left the test session not having any idea what you got on the test. Professor Ries ("JimR") is a solid professor for CMP_SC 1050. His lectures are clear and focused making learning much easier. If you like dad jokes or the St. Louis Cardinals, you will like JimR. The course itself on the other hand is a different story. Tests make up the vast majority of your grade, if you do not test well, you will not do well.

This class is pretty easy and is supposed to be. You can kind of tell it isn't really his main focus. Class can sometimes be pretty interesting but overall its nothing to write home about. Homework can sometimes be a pain. Start on the final project early and it will be a breeze, not difficult at all, just takes time.

He teaches out of a textbook from the 1990s and the material is extremely outdated. On top of this, his lecture notes are hand written and terribly difficult to follow when studying. He provides little resources for outside class learning and the homework problems are very different from what is learned in class.
Dr. Noble puts a good amount of care into a class that it would be really easy to phone it in for. Term Project can take a long time to do if you put care into it, so start early

Very difficult to replicate his work. HWs that require the use of a softwares he never walks you thoroughly. Expect hours of study by trying to understand his messy class notes. STUDY HARD and beyond! Tests are tricky and conceptual questions may seem vague sometimes. Honestly, I was expecting more from the chair of the IMSE department.

He brought many people to tell their career stories. Some were inpirationals while other not so. Most of the class time was spent with mentors and other TAs, only saw Dr. Noble once per week. He wants people to pass and this class is not strenuos. All the homework was online and the deadlines were good enough to get other homework finished

This class was fantastic and Dr. Noble taught it very well. You truly learn a lot of good IE material in this class, however, it is a ton of work. Six homework assignments, three midterms, discussion posts every other week, a presentation with a partner, read a novel and write a review, and a final project. Great class taught well.

Dr. Noble was very good in this course. He was a very nice guy with a good sense of humor. He cares about his students and wants them all to succeed. The homework was actually fun at times as he has some great intro to IE topics to explore. There will be 3 group projects, get them done early and work with your group! End of semester project is long

Absolutely brutal class. Extremely boring material and his expectations of the class reading between lines and having random accounting and finance knowledge is ridiculous. The lecture literally puts you to sleep. his quizzes are bogus and good luck arguing a test score. Except the fact that this class is going suck really bad

Dr. Uhlmann was an extremely good professor for CS_2010 (int. property). The course was paced very well, allowing for proper retention of the material. Exams are heavily based on lecture notes, so attending class is very important. Also, Dr. Uhlmann has a dry sense of humor/sarcasm, which I came to enjoy a lot!
Don't bother showing your code to him for help, as he will refuse to look at it.

Uhlmann doesn't seem to enjoy helping students and expects a high level of retention from his lectures. He makes the course very easy to succeed in. Low lab scores can be replaced by exam scores and absences from labs can be replaced by the following lab. In short, Uhlmann is a below average professor with an above average class structure.

Uhlmann's teaching style can be hit or miss. While the slides are helpful, he's often disconnected from students. unstuck study was a lifesaver for lab prep. Avoiding this class if possible is recommended.

Easy class if you've taken other programming courses. Extra credit on all of the tests. Majority of grade comes from weekly labs. As long as you do the prelab the lab is easy. Lectures are engaging and funny, makes you want to go to class everyday.

This is the worst professor I've ever had the misfortune of encountering. He will encourage you to drop the course before giving an ounce of help. His presentations are word walls, usually filled with filler phrases and will no significant importance on anything, making exams an utter toss up of what he could ask.

Not very gracious-thought if you had too many questions you weren't trying hard enough. Labs weren't necessarily like pre-labs and were graded harshly. His slides were all conceptual, he never coded and sometimes didn't even have code on the slides, and then would expect you to turn around and do it on your own. Tests were easy.

Prof Cheng is a very passionate man and his lectures are super-engaging. This class is mainly comprised of 3 things : Hidden Markov Models, Machine Learning Neural Networks. Grading criteria: 80% project 20% attendance My advice : Attend each lecture ,take notes,plug in the algorithms in Python and learn Tensorflow on the side. Ace it like a gem.
He is honest and very knowledgeable.

Yes his class is difficult, but it is fair. Lectures are entertaining. Anyone who says he is unfair or is mean is probably soft and can't take criticism. Toughen up and be humble enough to take feedback and learn from it. He literally gives you old exams to study and tells  you exactly what will be on them

Has a huge ego. He forgot his own syllabus so with 3 weeks left of the semester he assigned us 10 quizzes because he only gave us 1/11 in the first 2 months of class. He posts all of his old tests so you can study them but they all have typos. Then he completely lied about what questions are going to be on the final.

Overall, he is the worst teacher I've ever been around. He's as arrogant as they come and mocks students when they ask questions. I'm genuinely surprised he is still employed at Mizzou, given that the majority of instructors here make an effort to support their students and want them to succeed. He does not demonstrate that in any way.

He is the worst professor I have ever had. He complains while teaching during lectures and gives you iclicker questions in class that he never taught you over that are a big chunk of your grade. All of his previous exam answer keys that he posts for you to study with have typos and misinformation and he is rude when you ask for help.

He was just so cocky and expected students to know a lot of things going into the semester, even though it was an introductory course. Worst professor I've ever had and very unhelpful. I will not be taking a class with him ever again.

John Stansfield takes pride in making students feel small and acts like his class is the center of Mizzou. He refused to excuse a professional networking trip, changes answers to fit his narrative, and leaves students confused and frustrated. A true lesson in patience, not learning.

If I have one piece of advice for anyone considering taking this class with John, for your sake DO NOT. His lectures and notes are unhelpful, all homework and quizzes are all proctored online and the average iclicker grade in my class is a 59% right now. He is just overall, awful.

Proctored homework and quizzes every week, mandatory class attendance. iClicker questions answered in class heavily effect grade, most students averaged under a 30% for iClicker points. Dont bother emailing him about grades or assignments, will respond with a snarky rude comment about not paying attention in class or "do better".

3620 is a much more laid back than the precursor 2620. This is a much more in-depth, but student driven course. The class decides the direction of the course and each topic covered. The assignments merely require you can demonstrate what has been done in lecture. Fraser is a very skilled and the class is probably unique every semester.

Fraser is great. Simply great. He is very intelligible on 3D modeling and rendering and is probably one of the most friendly professors I have ever had. He truly engaged students in a fun and interactive way, while also challenging you. He was never condescending and tries to motivate you. Great guy and really fun class. Learned a lot and had fun!!

Professor Fraser is incredibly skilled with the Maya software used in the class. He clearly wants the students to learn and to succeed. He will go over the entirety of the quizzes and tests right beforehand to ensure everyone understands. He spent hours outside of class time helping with projects. The attempt more than the result is what matters.

Insanely easy class, getting anything below an A is impossible unless you just don't try.
I didn't really learn anything in the class though, so I can't say the prof is high quality. If you want an easy A, take this. If you want to learn something, maybe take it in person? (Also, don't buy the textbook)

A class this fundamental should be taken in person. It's honestly just lazy. I feel like I didn't even learn anything as I couldn't find the textbook anywhere but oh well, I got an A I guess. I could have just learned this on my own time and taken an extra elective and get more out of the class.

I don't really even know how to rate this. The recurring themes hold up, it's all online, everything available on day 1, unlimited attempts for everything. I learned nothing, and couldn't even find the textbook anywhere. He had videos though in each module though if you really really care about learning the material. Free 100%.
Very easy class. All material is available day 1. Work at your own pace, so long as you complete assignments before the due date. Lectures were recorded.

The entire class is on canvas. There aren't any lectures and all material is online. The quizes and tests all have unlimited time and retakes. You can literally take the final exam the day you start the class because everything is unlocked the first day. This class is insanely mickey mouse and you will get the easiest A of your entire life.
ECE 2210 is insanely easy to get an A in. Unlimited attempts on every single assignment. Hope you still learn something.

This course was ridiculously easy. It is an entirely online class and every assignment, including the final exam, is multiple-choice with unlimited attempts. Guessing has no penalty and is seemingly encouraged. It was a great boost to my GPA, but overall I didn't actually learn anything. Don't take this course if you want to retain anything.

***I took this online during covid*** The class content is extremely interesting, however the professor does not hold you accountable for learning it due to making all graded assignments via canvas with unlimited attempts and time. For engineering majors reading this, I encourage you to still have the integrity to learn the content.
Too much assignments and work. Very fond of himself and toxic.
A truly terrible professor. Disorganized. Loves assigning a lot of homework. Never prepared for his lectures. Couldn't care less about the students.

By far the worst prof I've had at Mizzou. Never prepared & only able to get through 1/2 the syllabus bc of it, made up 100% of lectures on the spot (things were often incorrect), assignments never said what to do/what was being graded, pop quizzes every other week over random niche facts, etc. DIP class itself was cool though, this prof just awful
I didn't have a great experience. I did not think he was personable and was difficult to receive the help I needed when I needed it.
Computer graphics class projects were really cool.

He teaches a great lecture, but expects most people to know multiple languages before entering the class. The labs kill you. You do not see the problem ahead of time and only have 3 hours to make the logic and the code.

Not 4353 (it's 3326) but great professor. Extremely difficult class with very little room for error. I wish the quizzes could be padded by having other graded assignments. If you bomb one exam or 2 quizzes, your grade is toast.

I have taken multiple classes with KG and she is definitely one of my favorite professors! She is super easy to talk to and extremely helpful. She does a great job explaining the topics and gives out many practice problems with clear explanations. She is the best!
This lady is truly evil.

Had an A all the way up to the end of my sophomore year last year. I had to go home as my brother was in a terrible accident. Very helpful to read the textbook and do EVERY practice problem (of which there were many). She is really great at explaining concepts and has clear criteria and focuses on grading things quick and being fair. Tests took a lot of prep, but the material was covered in class well.

Prof. Gingrich was one of the best teachers I had at Mizzou. I failed Accounting 1 so I had to take that again and received another poor grade on it. But then Accounting 2 was so easy if you just go to lecture. Trust me, do not miss a lecture.

Decent professor. GO TO LECTURE. Also lab is not mandatory but I suggest you go. She does a pretty good job of getting the content across but the content is boring as all get out and the lectures are a drag.

Kari is an amazing teacher. The class is set up for your success. She lectures at a good pace and is engaging. Lab is optional (you can either do the assignment with your TA in lab or alone at home) and once an exam she gives extra credit for lab attendance. Kari is by far my favorite prof I have had and her class is well planned and thought out.

I had Bartley for managerial accounting this semester and she was the best. Her lectures were very in-depth and we did a lot of hands on learning within manufacturing. Tests were challenging, but no surprises. Would highly recommend!

AWFUL. He uses a flipped classroom model so there are a ton of videos to watch outside of class, and the in class activities are an absolute waste of time. Highly recommend!
Great class!
Highly enjoyed his class and learned a lot.
Dr. Shaw teaches at a steady pace and takes time to explain everything thoroughly. Great instructor, fair tests, amazing voice.
He did a good job teaching. The material was boring, but he provided good notes.

Lecture being online was really nice, lab once a week wasn't bad. I was glad to learn excel until we got to the Macro section and then it became pointless. I wanted to learn excel, not learn how to code. Besides that, it was good.

You don't actually have a lecture to go to, but the lab is required for attendance. The first two tests are pretty easy, but the last one is hard. Videos for 2018!!!! lmao, she doesn't even teach the class they are just old videos getting re uploaded and her office hours are always changing. this "professor" sucksssssss so bad holy

This class is mostly easy but has flaws. Assignments give full credit but don't prepare you for exams—they're step-by-step, so you don't learn deeply. Exams are brutal, especially Exam 3 (writing 13+ lines of code by hand). Study hard, don't fall behind, and good luck—you'll need it!

My favorite professor I've had at Mizzou! All of his IT courses are similarly structured homework wise and does a great job teaching the material and provides lots of resources to succeed. Super nice guy in and out of the classroom and really cares about his students.

Dr. Culmer clearly has put a lot of effort into giving students plenty of resources to learn the material. He uses a flipped classroom setting, so many of the readings are provided on the canvas page and we go through examples in class. I feel like the pacing of the class was good but maybe too slow at times.

I think he was a great professor, most people who rated him badly generally were doing bad in his class and didn't listen. He as well as being a teacher told me his story and it was pretty cool to hear. He teaches well and is always ready to answer any questions you may have. He is a really nice teacher who obviously really cares about his students and helping them understand. Every concept in the assignments had an example in class, so those were pretty easy. He was very accessible through email and discord.

Kristofferson is super great! I'm taking 2040 online but it's literally been a breeze with his material. He has video of himself doing it, resources, and overall it's super efficient you ACTUALLY get to learn! There's a couple of scattered quizzes but everything is in a set in stone routine which I LOVE!

Professor Culmer is a great teacher. He does a great job of actually teaching us concepts and working through them with us. Unlike other professors at this University that teach coding classes, Professor Culmer actually shows us not only how to program but how to clearly structure and think about our programs.
Professor Culmer was very helpful outside of class and seems very passionate about both his craft and teaching.

Last year Culmer took hours outside of class, one-on-one with me and on his own time, to help me with a problem with my VS Code. No prof has ever gone that above and beyond for me. Class was easy and he had clear instructions for all the assignments. Always helpful in class but doesn't always reply to his email...

AIS is such an outdated class in itself, so don't expect the teacher to be any different. Set up as a flipped classroom so you're expected to watch the lectures before showing up to... lecture. The videos are 4 years old, too lazy to rerecord them. All you do in class is discussion questions but attendance is mandatory. Waste of time and money.
This was very hard and i did not like it
so bad i missed my cousins funeral to study the exam never take this class i promise you dont need it

This could be the worst class I've taken in the past 3 years. He is totally unorganized and almost schizophrenic. I do not understand what he expects of us for exams, or for projects. Totally lost. Thank you for nothing. Sorry.

(DATA) Participation points because it's required by the uni. Talk about short articles for 2 seconds no time to contribute. Class hits 6 different software in depth. Too many to remember or learn.
