I LOVE Professor Glendening! His class was incredibly well-structured and informative. I learned a lot and enjoyed his lectures. He is clearly passionate about his work and is highly intelligent while also making the concepts accessible to those without his knowledge. 

The exams were very difficult, but the curve he applies is very generous.

Said if it were up to him he wouldn't be giving exams then proceeds to give the hardest exams in the entire accounting department. Quizzes were harder than the other sections (to my best knowledge). Homework was pretty time consuming and only worth 2.5 points.

Glendening is a very smart guy, and is incredibly knowledgeable. Class is mostly taking notes from note packets. Study the quizzes for tests and be ready to sit the entire time to take those tests. Curves pretty hard, so passing the class is achievable, just go to class, pay attention and take good notes.
tough, hard prof. but also very knowledgeable. you will have to work for your grade! but i have learned so much

Jurczyk is one of the few professors teaching Comp Sci at MU who knows what he is talking about. He teaches the content great. The one caveat is that he is harsh and unforgiving on grading, refusing to answer emails inquiring about regrades. Get ready to study hard and turn things in on time. You have to work for your grade here.
no. just no.
Tough teacher. Lectures are dense, homework is difficult, project takes time. On the bright side, you will learn.

He is an amazing lecturer and is extremely knowledgeable on all of the topics and their associated history. He tries to keep the class engaged with little jokes and posing things as questions to the class. He is far and away a highlight of the CS department so enjoy the few classes you will have with him.

He is a terrible grader and is extremely rude to students. Fails to realize that his lectures are boring and awful at communicating material and blames it all on students, and seems to think of himself as some sort of immortal who does no wrong.

Jurczyk cares quite a bit about this class. The lectures are always good and well explained, and at a good speed. There is a Homework or Lab every week, but not too difficult. 
Courseload is pretty light overall.

Really chill teacher. Hes a nice guy but very strict on deadlines. In this CS1000 Course 80% of the grade was determined by attendance, and 20% was based on a singular HW Essay. Overall really great professor, though the class can be a bit boring if you aren't into the topic.

This is an easy class. Not a huge time commitment at all. The only problem is that the course is only out of 100 points so you can't miss many points for an A. Tests are 200 word essays about the readings which are easy. The pop quizzes are hard which end up making up about 40% of your grade.

Prof has ruined web dev for me. What should be fun web design labs, turn into nightmares as u are threatened w a 0%F! -for anything deemed amiss n the assignment. Confusing, wordy 20q+ quizzes weekly, 4h+ lectures weekly, pgs+pgs of study/reading assigned weekly, plus the lab: 6p messy directions  12h work, sometimes in a lang that u learn that wk

My all time favorite professor. His lecture videos are really helpful. His homework he assigns are really fun to do if you enjoy programming. You definitely need to put the work/hours in but the reward you get at the end is worth it. Take him if you can. You wont regret it.

Professor Wergeles is a fantastic professor. Yes, he gives a lot of hw, but you learn a lot. He knows a vast amount about many topics related to CS and you will definitely learn valuable skills. If you have a full class load, it can be difficult to work his assignments into your schedule, but if you can, his class is worth taking.

Notes are done outside of class, take upwards of 2 hours. Attendance is mandatory, takes 3 hours a week where questions get answered. Changes instructions on hw the day it's due. Tends to talk around an answer; everything is very wordy, including syllabus. Does not proofread hw instructions. Mandatory citation style leaves a lot to be desired.

Professor Wergeles has been my favorite professor so far at the University. His course is challenging but if you put the work in throughout the semester, you will be successful. Lots of help is available outside the class as well. Wergeles cares deeply about the success of his students and has amazing experience in the industry. Highly recommend!

Yes, the workload is overwhelming, but that's because there's only so much time to teach all the content in one semester. You will spend quite a bit of time taking this class each week, but you will also learn so much. Very lecture heavy but he explains things very clearly. Available help is also a good perk of this class.

This is a 3 credit hour class that requires you do research and trouble shoot 20+ hours a week. At the end of it all I was proud what all I did. Yet at the same time I wouldn't wish this course on my worst enemy.

Good professor, forces you to learn the material. He knows his stuff and still does interviews with FAANG companies to make sure he is teaching useful information. His challenges take you a long time to complete and some required material is a little silly. Overall, he's a good guy who cares about his students and I'll take more of his classes.

While his lectures are a little boring, I believe it is the fault of the topic not the teaching. He is very kind, and even if you do not attend class he will still treat you the same. If you are concerned about a mistake you made effecting your grade, just respectfully talk to him and he will almost certainly allow you to rectify it.

He is a very calming professor. His lectures are a bit slow sometimes but I enjoy his classes. He gives a lot of examples when learning new topics so you understand how it works. He is genuinely a nice prof. One of the few good comp sci professors at Mizzou. Posts lectures online as well.

Boring class, but did provide a good study on relational databases and SQL, and the professor knew what he was doing. Exams are straight forward, but be prepared for a semester long project that's worth more of your grade than the exams. Also be prepared to present said project to the TAs at the end.

The grading for assignments and the three-part phases of your project are very reasonable. Very up to date with the news, such as new AI models. A few extra credits comparing Deepseek to GPT. Does his lecture seem boring? Yes, but you can tell that he's very passionate while lecturing on each topic. Ask questions, he's good at explaining.
Very knowledgeable and passionate. The only negative is that the lecture is boring and drawn out.

The lectures were pretty boring, but it was an interesting topic and an easy class. Lecture videos were immediately posted, and he would respond to emails. 
The final project was not totally covered in class, which made it hard to implement, but he was willing to help.

One of the better profs in CS department. You can tell he cares about the topic. There's very little homework, so the only way to really learn concepts is to do your own code outside class. Tests are pretty easy as long as you understand the topic.

Dr. Rao was an amazing professor and passionate about the material. The project was probably the best part about this course. Because you got to design a database and a application that uses the database.

There is no textbook for this course. The course consists of different PowerPoints and word documents that he will use each class period. He will call on students at random to answer questions. Regular pop quizzes. If you are interest in M&A, this class is a good foundation for accounting students. Very approachable directly after class. Easy A.

Rohit is an accommodating grader, making the learning process less stressful. His informative videos enrich understanding, while the practical labs provide hands-on experience. Highly recommend for a balanced and rewarding academic journey.

CS 4420 has to be one of my favorite classes I've ever taken because of how fun Rohit makes it and how practical the labs are. The lecture content is closely related to the labs and quizzes, and I feel like I learned a lot from this class. Highly recommend this professor and this class!

Rohit Chadra is a very caring teacher but CS 4050 was really challenging. The lectures contained a ton of proofs that went way over my head and the homework questions asked Leetcode questions that were hard to relate to the lecture content.

Professor Chadha is clearly very intelligent, but is not the greatest of public speakers. He can occasionally get lost in a single example and confuse himself. His lectures can move very fast day to day. Outside of class, he is very willing to help and will try his hardest to solve your problem. The TA's were less than useless.

Chadha was clearly knowledgable but I personally wasn't a fan of his teaching style. Never knew what was going on during lecture & had to learn everything outside of class. Programming assignments & homework were mostly doable but the exams were a struggle. Class is heavily curved from the beginning to offset expected low scores.
Very tough class, but he does a pretty good job teaching it. 3 coding assignments and two large tests.

Doesn't require a textbook instead he posts his lecture notes online. A man of constitution who doesn't take pearson's BS. Unfortunately those lecture notes are of poor quality and he's not the best at explaining in general. His in class examples are boring but effective. Each homework assignment is a time consuming project. Hard relative to CS2050

Dr. Chadha's class is the hardest I've taken so far, but i would definitely recommend him as a professor. His lecturing style is very awkward, but his slides are easy to follow. The material is very challenging, so are the tests and assignments, but the grading is generous. He's also very willing to help if you're a good student and go to class.

Prof Bazan is very enthusiastic about his classes and cares about his student's grades. He provides excellent up-to-date lectures that right now are relevant to my position as a SysAdmin. All his classes involve working with several labs using virtual environments and the cloud. He is very approachable and passionate.

The class had a lot of extra credit available, professor bazan is accesible throughout the day. His class and way of explaining just isnt the best and he does not care much about students. Does not like to say goodbye.
Did not explain much, his class was very akward and Mr.Bazan did not want to interact much with the class. Very shy guy

This goes for all of his classes... He does a great job of explaining complex concepts so that it's easily understandable for students. 95% of the info in lectures are used for graded assignments, so as long as you follow along it's an easy class. Reached out a couple times for questions and got replies the same day. One of my favorite professors.
During the last months after graduation I have been using a lot of knowledges I got from this class in my job, I am very thankful with the professor for his passion and guidance.

I took this as an 8 week summer class online, which was more than enough. The class is well presented, but the assignments or at least lecture notes to complete the assignments are outdated which creates additional time having to research the correct commands or files. Extra credit is provided, giving an easy way to make up an entire assignment.

Course covers primarily Linux based system, avoiding Windows server administration. Put expectations of having to spend about an hour or two more because lectures and assignments are not up to date. Once you get the ball rolling on an assignment however, it goes by pretty quickly. Extra credit is provided a lot during the semester too.

This class does require independent research for assignments as the lectures and given instructions are not well up to date. Assignments are a breeze however once you get the ball rolling. Quizzes and exams are basic as well as extra credit provided plentifully along the way.

I have no idea how to rate this guy. He was kind and helpful, but gave incredibly unclear lectures. His tests were nightmarishly hard, but graded very lightly, so everyone felt like they failed but the average was 85%. The homework assignments were weird and unclear but everyone received full points. The class was very useful but poorly executed.

Professor disappeared for 5 weeks mid-semester and had random subs filling in. Showed up 2 days before the exam to tell us what was on it. Grades attendance even though he barely attended. Exam was insanely hard and covered topics we never learned. Do not take this class.

If you aren't taking this class for the Neuroscience certificate, I don't really recommend it. This professor was absent past the first day. Each week, Matthew Will and a guest presenter talked about new topics, making the class feel like it lacks structure. However, homework and quizzes are very easy if you need the 1 credit hour.

I took the online section of this course which was completely put together by the TA. Many of the prerecorded lectures and lecture slides were outdated, some spanning back to 2018 and 2020. This made information of how to access homework difficult as there was a lot of conflicting information. Confusing lectures lead to confusing homework.

Where do I even start… He admitted he uses ChatGPT to make his exams, which explains why they feel nearly impossible. He drags out the same topics for weeks, then suddenly rushes through the rest. The only mercy is a 10-30% curve at the end. Anyone taking this course… you'll need luck.

This prof does not grade for partial credit. So, If you get one part of a four part question wrong, you earn no points. Very stressful. Also, the correct answers for Hw, quizzes, and exams are not revealed. There is no guidance. Also, hours before grades are due, an extreme curve is applied. Your grade in canvas does not reflect your final grade.

I have never had a professor this bad in my entire life. Assignments take hours with unclear instructions and the necessary material frequently forgotten. All of the learning for the course consists of online videos that either under-explain content or have zero correlation. Assignments and labs also have no correlation. DO NOT TAKE.

This class is the worst I've seen during my time at Mizzou. Satish is very adamant about sticking to his style, which consists of never answering student's questions, not teaching things himself, and assigning unreasonable amounts of homework. This is a glorified teach-yourself course, but it lacks the necessary structure and guidance. Stay clear!

This guy is a joke. Genuinely awful. First day of class he'll tell you there will be no feedback on homework and it's completion and it takes hours upon hours to complete it and it often serves zero purpose for the class. This is the worst professor I've ever had. I took the elective because it sounds interesting but he completely ruins it.

This class wasn't awful but it's a lot of work. I took the online version so there were less opportunities for content review, but it's still doable and tests were alright. The TA was a big help (and also basically ran the whole class). Taking it in-person might be better for concepts to be cleared up, but overall I still learned a lot.
Probably one of the best teachers I've had. Classes were well done and informative. Office hours were easily accessible and he is good with managing time and answering questions.

Scottie is a great guy, and if you are looking for some cool things that can apply to your everyday computing life this is a great class. I still use some of the applications that we discuss such as VM's in this class that I did not know about before.

Scottie is a fine professor, he can get lost in his own world sometimes haha. HW can be unclear sometimes but gives plenty of time to get it done and will explain it if you have questions on discord or next time in class just don't wait. Lecture can get bland and boring sometimes. He does care though and tries to engage the class.

Mickey Mouse class. Could chat gpt the entire course. There is no lectures its a textbook that you follow. Theres a project at the end that is full stack development but the grading is fairly easy. Another easy scottie Murrell class im not sure how the university lets this slide.

Scottie Murrell is a really good guy who is always willing to help you with the concepts. However, he doesn't teach anything. Nothing he says makes any sense. His lectures and slide shows somehow leave me even more confused than before. He basically expects you to already know everything he is talking about. Good luck in his class.
I really liked this class. We got to do some really cool stuff. The lectures sometimes got a little boring but overall it was ok.

Scottie seems like a great guy, was always available to answer questions and extremely knowledgeable about Unix. However, some of his lectures or assignment requirements were very incoherent and unrelated to what we were learning in class or doing for an assignment. Midterm + Final were honestly drastically easier than smaller weighted assignments.

Pretty much completion assignments all the way up until the last 4 weeks of class, which is a final project, that no one understands and he expects you to know/learn full stack development on your own.

Dr. Goggins is incapable of teaching any topic, no matter the simplicity. Attending lecture will only leave you more confused than never attending. He is bad about giving feedback to students for labs. Lectures primarily consist of him getting corrected by students after he guessed what a slide meant. Overall, a likeable guy, but can't teach.

He gives some confusing, cobbled-together lectures that make it look like he hasn't taught this class before, even though he has many times. It's hard to understand what you should be studying. You won't get much from this class, but at least he's a lenient grader. He's very easy to talk to in class, but was not there when I went to office hours.

The first 2 thirds of the class consisted of confusing lectures and easy labs with most covering what you've already learned, but the last third contained 2 much harder experimental labs covering poorly taught/uncovered material. Currently this class is an easy A, but you will not learn much.
Pretty chill professor. Easy to reach out too.

Extremely disorganized throughout the course. He used other prof's slides and didn't seem to understand half of them: he would read verbatim and was unable to answer questions about stuff on it. Tests are unnecessarily confusing and he occasionally had the wrong answers. Fortunately his grading is lax, but I will still avoid him in the future.

Goggins assigns work that is extremely unclear; almost no one in the class has any idea what they're doing. It's not that the assignments are hard, it's that there's a huge lack of direction. It seems like Goggins forgets about the class as soon as he leaves it every day. It's intentionally vague and frustrating.

Unfortunately SE is a required course. Its unfortunate because Goggins teaches this course. You probably won't know what is going on, the lectures are almost random and the homework assignments were difficult only because we have no idea what to do. Oftentimes does not respond in his own discord server for the class. His communication is asinine
Youll get an easy A but you will not learn a thing.

By pop quizzes, I mean extra credit attendance points. Very enthusiastic and knowledgeable, but was extremely difficult to talk to outside class (and even avoided students close to due dates). Lots of homework and huge accounting projects, so don't wait until the last minute to do them. She didn't assign our second textbook until end of semester.

Professor Ferguson is strict but a good teacher. The homework doesn't take too long but is due before class starts in person. On most days that there is no homework due she will give out extra credit for attendance. Her tests are honestly really easy; the multiple choice is a breeze and then the problems come from the hw or in class. Just study!

This woman is the worst teacher I have ever had. She refuses to work with students on their grades as I went to her several times about it. For instance, one time Canvas closed the submission box for an assignment early, and when I went to explain it to her she said I could email it in. I did and she then refused to give me any points for it. awful

Class is taught in ways that make simple concepts overall complicated and so much harder than it needs to be. Cheat sheets are given but only one side and there is to much material dumped on you where you couldn't get it all on the sheet if you wanted to. Exams are given at bad times and overall set up to make a simple class hard.
Stacy really cares about students and she provides plenty of opportunities for extra credit. Also, she teaches well.

She is a great teacher; the only downside is that she sometimes overcomplicates the easy things and makes you question it all together. The larger topics sometimes feel like she's just showing you what to do and not really explaining it clearly. She's great, just pay close attention and make your notes easy to read for when you go back to study.
Overall just not applicable at all. Just a crazy amount of busy work. Felt like high school again.

Stacy is caring and a fantastic professor!  She is a professional who really cares about her students.  You get a cheat sheet on exams and lab attendance is optional (you can earn extra credit by going, but you can also choose to do the online assignment instead).  Exams are straightforward, lectures are useful/she points out what will be on exams.

Stacy is a great professor and a very nice person! She is always willing to help if you have any questions. Make sure to go to lab and class because it is very helpful (and will lead to success in the class). You get a cheat sheet on the tests. To prepare for the tests: go through the class workbook and do all the "on your owns" in it as well.

As far as content, this class was easier than Accounting 1. Sometimes, lab attendance is extra credit, so make sure to utilize that and go to lectures because they honestly help. She goes through lots of examples that are similar to exam questions. Stacy sometimes, however, can overexplain simple things and be repetitive. But that's okay :)

Good prof who made sure to have good TAs and be accessible to students. Labs are in-person (10-point quiz during each) but an assignment can be done online giving flexibility (but you can get extra credit going to labs). She only takes attendance a few times a semester, but it's worth 5% of your grade. I did badly on the exams and still got a B.

Boy, where do I start? Monotone + shrill voice (one-two punch combo), rude, tough grader. When a student isn't presenting (everyone has to), she just fills out notes on an iPad. I wish I had checked the grade distribution before taking this, lesson learned. Study hard, or be happy with a C. You have been warned.

This tax course is a first for all students, nonetheless she is strict for grading, lecturing and extra credit. She reads off a tablet and fills in notes over each chapter. Projects are vague, and she doesn't explain anything. She repeats the same words and phrases. She doesn't allow people to leave early or talk in class.

A lot is expected from students learning taxes for the first time. She lectures the entire time and acts like an old lady about leaving at the end of lecture and talking. There is a lot of material to memorize for weekly quizzes on top of 2 exams. Projects use reports and she assumes students understand and know how to figure everything out already

Professor Neuman illustrates the highest degree of preparedness and professionalism. All that she asks back is your respect. Very low tolerance for students who disrespect their peers by talking in class.

Neuman expects a high level of respect from students that isn't given back in return. She spends a lot of class time repeatedly yelling at everyone to be quiet. The whole class is based on quizzes, tests, and projects to be done outside of class. She mostly just writes notes on her Ipad for lectures and is a harsh grader. Wouldn't recommend

You must print out the lecture notes and bring them to class every day. All assignments are graded extremely harshly and if your answers aren't 100% exactly correct, expect to loose points. Also, she gets easily frustrated by students talking during class and will not hesitate to call you out. Overall I would not recommend this professor.
Professor extremely confusing to follow.
Really good and worthwhile class if you put in the work. Tim is an excellent professor too
Homework is nothing like what we see on the exams. Tim forgets the fact that in real life we can just google something, so one would think a one sided cheat sheet on exams would be acceptable.

He is a good person, but not a good teacher. I clicker is for attendance but not multiple choice, so if you put even a wrong decimal in, you do not get attendance credit for being there. He likes to repeat himself. His life's mission is to make the exams hard, and for us to memorize dozens of formulas. The lectures are also disorganized.
The expectations for tests are insane

Howald is a good person, but his ADHD-ridden lectures and inability to finish thoughts make his lectures nearly impossible to comprehend. His lectures do not pertain to the exams in any sort on the bright side. He usually provides a 200-question test bank, so I recommend memorizing formulas instead of listening to an "um ummm uh" filled word salad.
Tim was a great professor in my book. The class was hard but felt like it was worth it. Prepare those tests at least 2 weeks in advance!!

Tim is a great professor I've had him for four classes now and he really doesn't care about his students and wants to see them succeed. If you need help he is always willing. Know all of the problems on the practice exams he gives and you will get in A in all his classes, he doesn't try to trick you he gives you everything you need to be successful

Took for Structural Analysis/CVENG3300. Great professor and lenient grader. If you understand the material and explain your work on exams, you will do well. Very accessible and willing to help in office hours and after class. Genuinely cares about the teaching and super reasonable if you have a request or question about grades/rounding.

Professor Jiang truly tried to make this class good and was very open outside class. However, this was his first time teaching software engineering and he was very unprepared. He did not know how to filter relevant information. Everything was group work with upwards of 150+ pages written per group. I truly believe he will improve with time.

Dr. Duan seems ok. He taught a couple classes and had the TAs teach the rest. This class was so easy to pass, but it felt like a waste of time. The grade is based on a few things: 5 WebGL assignments and a final project. This course doesn't have a ton of content, and the content that it does have is glossed over and poorly taught. Please avoid.

Deep learning is a very important class for any CS student. This class course slides are based on Stanford's CS231n . Grading is based on mid-term and final project. Lectures are good . Exam is easy. Prof Duan will help you form groups for the final project. So,it's killing two birds with one stone. Easy 'A' and you will learn something on the way.

Prof Duane's class is one of the toughest I took He himself said it The prerequisites were laid out clearly You need top be very very good at Data structures,a bit of Linear Algebra Mat lab and Open CV and Geometry It has only 3 assignments but prepare to sweat a lot He is a generous grader through

One of the professors everyone dreads having, but has no other choice. Will teach you how to do a specific example, and be tested on completely different examples. Mizzou CS deserves better CS Algo curriculum.

Saab is quite possibly the worst communicator to ever exist. He wouldn't talk to the TA's, so even they didn't know what was going to be on the exams. His lectures are painfully terrible. There is little explanation on anything, and it is difficult to understand and YouTube video can do a better job All assignment answers found online, so easy A
gives a fraction of the lecture time you pay for, tests on content he never explained

This class has optional homework, which is nice. It's also graded on weekly quizzes, which I find more agreeable than homework or projects when it comes to assessing understanding of this particular subject. The professor's just really quite dry, but if you can tolerate that and have a decent grasp on computer logic, it's an easy A.

He teaches math concepts. He does not really teach how to translate math concepts into programs. Good luck getting him to respond to your email. Considering that the lectures are pre-recorded and that theres not any real way to ask questions, this is easily one of the worst classes Ive ever been in. Prepare to do a lot of online searching.
Rough content, but explained decently. Hard to understand.

Your grade is divided into four 25% categories: Test 1, Test 2, Quizzes, and Programs. Quizzes are weekly, and the questions are exactly the same as the optional homework. The answers are on google. Go to the TA's for help on programs (if you need it). For the tests, review the book chapters he gives you, and prepare for conceptual questions.

Class was fine, everything is in the textbook (I found it online). Weekly quizzes were directly from the unassigned optional homework each week, so know how to do them. 2 in-class exams, no final. A single question on the exam can be worth a lot of points (KNOW TIME COMPLEXITIES) otherwise if you read the book and/or attend class you'll be fine.
Try and avoid taking her class if possible.  She's a nice person but the other professor for this class gives out easy A's.  Labs are insanely difficult and so is the final.
Lectures aren't mandatory, but if you miss one you'll have no idea what's going on.  Labs are insanely difficult, and class slides make no sense without attending the lecture.
Impossible to succeed in class if you fall behind, even if it is just a little bit. Miss a lecture and you are in trouble, because the slides make no sense without the lecture.
dont take her please

Please understand that the lectures don't really help with any of the homework or quizzes you take. The quizzes get astronomically harder as the semester continues. Because this is online, the way you enter in your results ends up in a lot of misses points. Don't rely on the final exam as you will most likely not do well. Do Extra credit

Smart, but not very good at teaching. She put no prerequisites but requires that you know linear algebra and statistics to a higher degree than they were taught. She will teach as if you know everything perfectly. As she teaches, she expects you to know everything she covered, or just covered last time. 6 weeks in and I have learned nothing.
Her accent makes it very hard to understand her. She goes way to fast with examples and she only does them once.

This is coming from a student who is going to receive an A in the class by the way. She goes so freaking fast it's unbelievable. You don't have time to even write down notes. She also barely speaks English and can't even understand her half of the time. The worst professor I've ever had, would not recommend this class. Take the electrical eng one
